svg.text: Landing zone setup — manual vs. automated

g.text: One-time setup. Done by a senior architect or cloud lead. ~1 day.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Walk me through setting up the AWS Organizations root account manually')
sendPrompt('How do you set up the IaC bootstrap account?')
sendPrompt('What IAM role does the IaC pipeline need?')

svg.text: Also: enable CloudTrail org-wide, set billing alerts, enable MFA on root
This is the ONLY phase where console clicks are acceptable

g.onclick: sendPrompt('Show me a Terraform module for AWS Organizations OU structure')
sendPrompt('What SCPs should be applied via Terraform for banking?')
sendPrompt('How do you automate Transit Gateway and VPC creation?')
sendPrompt('How do you set up IAM Identity Center via Terraform?')

svg.text: Also: Security Hub, GuardDuty, Config Rules, Log Archive bucket policies
CI/CD pipeline, container registry, artifact repository
All changes via PR → plan → approve → apply. No console changes.

g.text: Repeatable, self-service. Each new workload follows a template.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does account vending work with Terraform?')
sendPrompt('What does a workload VPC Terraform module look like?')
sendPrompt('How are EKS clusters provisioned via IaC?')

svg.text: Account vending machine: team requests → PR → auto-provision account,
VPC, TGW attachment, IAM roles, CI/CD pipeline — all from modules
